fact: March 13 is exactly 72 days into the year - that's the same number of squares on a checkerboard!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is when maple trees start getting their sap sucked out to make syrup - they're basically tree vampires!
March 13 falls smack in the middle of Pisces season - the fish zodiac sign that makes people extra dreamy and weird.
Earthworms typically start wiggling up to the surface around this time in northern climates because the ground is finally thawing - it's like a slimy underground party!
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, March 13 is when the air starts getting crisp and leaves begin their colorful autumn change.
The sun rises about 2 minutes earlier each day around March 13 in most places - that's 14 extra minutes of daylight just this week!
Frogs in cold places are starting to wake up from their winter mud naps and getting ready to croak their hearts out for love.
March 13 is when dandelions in warmer areas start plotting their takeover of every lawn - those yellow invaders are sneaky!
Bears in northern forests are getting grumpy and hungry as they start waking up from hibernation - imagine being hangry for 5 months!
Around March 13, daylight hours finally beat nighttime hours in most of the Northern Hemisphere - light wins the daily battle!
